However, I found information on the plant "Rhaponticum exaltatum" which is also known as "Rhaponticum" or "Russian Rhaponticum." Here is a paragraph about the plant:

Rhaponticum exaltatum is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa, known for its medicinal and adaptogenic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to improve overall health and well-being, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, as well as improved immune function and cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Rhaponticum exaltatum include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, as well as supplements in the form of capsules or tablets.
taxonomy: Rapuntium exaltatum, also known as the exalted meadow sedge, belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This family is commonly known as the sedge family and contains approximately 5,000 species of plants. Rapuntium exaltatum is a type of sedge that is native to North America. The genus Rapuntium is a synonym of Carex, which is a large and diverse genus of sedges. The species Rapuntium exaltatum is often considered a synonym of Carex exaltata.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
